data.name: Apex - Site Safety Inspection Report - New

entries.label: Apex supervisors and/or management are required to conduct a minimum two Site Safety Inspections annually on project sites; however, all employees are strongly encouraged to perform Site Safety Inspections monthly for long term projects (greater than 2 weeks) and once per project for short term projects (less than 2 weeks).
Comments and corrective actions are required for all negative (No) responses.
Project Name
Project Number
Inspected By
Date
Office
*** Please select "CSW" if you are in the Commercial Stormwater Division ***
Division
Apex Employees On-Site
Type of Work Performed
Supervisor
Supervisor Email
Site Specific HASP available on site for review?
Explanation
HASP signed by all Apex employees?
Explanation
Phone no./directions for the nearest medical center available?
Explanation
Have JSAs been prepared for each task?
Explanation
Are action levels established for known contaminants?
Explanation
Confirm no contaminated environmental media onsite
Are the correct breathing zone monitors onsite?
Explanation
Confirm no potential for airborne contaminant exposure
Are the monitors calibrated properly?
Explanation
Are tailgate meetings held and documented daily?
Explanation
Are tailgate meeting forms signed by all contractors and Apex employees?
Explanation
Confirm no contractors onsite
Are copies of past meetings maintained in the project files/HASP?
Explanation
Confirm this is the first day onsite
Are first aid supplies readily accessible?
Explanation
Client contact information available?
Explanation
Are SDS's available for materials/chemicals brought on site?
Explanation
Confirm no chemicals brought to the site
General work area neat and organized?
Explanation
Equipment/Supplies stored properly?
Explanation
Confirm that no tools or supplies are being used onsite
Are slip, trip and fall hazards identified and removed?
Explanation
Confirm no slip, trip and fall hazards present onsite
G.O.A.L. being implemented?
Explanation
Will Apexians be exposed to contaminated environmental media?
Are actions levels established for monitoring and PPE upgrades?
Explanation
Respirators available for PPE upgrades?
Explanation
Confirm valid fit test for all
Confirm that HASP does not require Level C PPE Upgrade
Are appropriately selected chemical-resistant gloves used?
Explanation
Confirm that no hand hazards present
Hard hats are being worn?
Explanation
Confirm no overhead hazards
Safety glasses are being worn?
Explanation
Confirm no particulate hazards
Hearing protection being worn when required?
Explanation
Confirm no noise requiring raised voices to be heard
Traffic vests being worn?
Explanation
Confirm no vehicular or equipment traffic
Safety shoes are being worn?
Explanation
Ground fault circuit interrupters being used?
Explanation
Confirm no powered equipment used without GFCI outlet
Are flexible cords and cables protected from damage?
Explanation
Confirm no cord or cables being used
Inspect all extension cords. Any damage observed?
Confirm that all damaged cords are removed from service
Confirm no extension cords being used
Are hand and/or power tools being used or are available for use onsite?
Inspect all tools.  Any damaged tools or missing guards?
Confirm that all damaged tools are removed from service and missing guards are replaced
Confirm no tools onsite
Observe use of power tools.  Are all guards and safety equipment in place?
Confirm all guards are replaced or tool removed from service
Confirm no tools onsite
Right tool being available/used for job?
Explanation
Confirm that no tools needed for job
Are ladders being used onsite or in/on an Apex Truck?
Inspect all ladders. Are ladders damaged?
Confirm that all damaged ladders are removed from service
Confirm no ladders onsite
Ladders extend at least 36 inches above the support location?
Explanation
Confirm no straight ladders used onsite
Are ladders positioned at the proper angle (4:1 ratio)?
Explanation
Confirm no straight ladders being used onsite
Stepladders and A-Frame used in fully open position?
Explanation
Confirm no straight ladders being used onsite
Ladders are secured to prevent slipping, sliding, or falling?
Explanation
Confirm no straight ladders used onsite
Are any ground disturbance activities being conducted onsite?
Is a Ground Disturbance permit available for review?
Explanation
Has 8-1-1 or local equivalent been contacted?
Explanation
Has a Private Utility Locate been performed?
Confirm approval received from division manager to waive private locate
Are proposed disturbance areas physically cleared to 5-feet?
Explanation
Are proposed disturbance areas physically cleared 25% greater than downhole equipment?
Explantion
Will excavations be installed onsite?
Will Apex employees operate heavy equipment onsite?
Operator competency – Please detail training/experience of each operator
Will Apex employees enter open excavations?
Please describe sloping and shoring plan
Is the excavation a permit-required confined space?
Please explain PRCS plan and verify training for entrants and attendants
Competent person on site?
Explain
Excavations over 5 feet in depth are shored or sloped back?
Explain
Confirm all excavations are less than 5-feet deep
Materials are stored at least 2 feet from excavation edge?
Explain
Confirm no soil piles or material storage onsite
Equipment is a safe distance from edge of trench or excavation?
Explain
Confirm no equipment within 6-feet of any excavation
Ladders provided within 25 feet of all work areas within the excavation that is more than 4 feet deep?
Explain
Confirm ladders are not required for egress from any excavation
Are employees required to lift more than 50 pounds by themselves?
Explain why and give examples of alternative methods
Materials are properly stored or stacked?
Explain
Confirm no materials stored or stacked onsite
Employees are using proper lifting methods?
How were lifting methods corrected while onsite?
Tag lines are used to guide loads?
Explain
Proper number of workers for each operaton?
Explain
Item observed: (e.g., Best management practices, Unsafe acts, Unsafe conditions, Safety policy violations, Near hits, Incidents)

Photo
